prompts.text: AI alignment is technically solvable before AGI arrives
Recursive self-improvement will lead to a rapid intelligence explosion
Current AI systems already pose meaningful alignment risks
Interpretability research alone is sufficient to solve AI alignment
We should expect misaligned AI to behave deceptively before deployment
AI development should be paused until safety is better understood
Open-source AI models do more harm than good
Governments should have direct oversight of frontier AI labs
Racing dynamics between AI labs make catastrophic outcomes more likely
International AI governance treaties are achievable and effective
Existential risk from AI is the most pressing problem facing humanity today
Short-term AI harms deserve more attention than long-term existential risk
P(doom) from AI exceeds 10%
The AI Safety community is too focused on speculative long-term risks
Misaligned superintelligence is a more likely catastrophe than misuse by humans
Frontier Safety Frameworks are security theater
prompts.subtitle: 12 companies published or updated Frontier Safety Frameworks in 2025, but the International AI Safety Report notes they vary widely in the risks they cover. Are self-reported safety docs meaningless without external enforcement?

prompts.text: AI labs that accept government defense contracts forfeit the right to call themselves safety-focused
prompts.subtitle: If your alignment research is funded by the same entity deploying your model for lethal autonomous systems, is the mission compromised?

prompts.text: A rogue AI is less dangerous than an obedient one in the wrong hands
prompts.subtitle: The AI safety field has historically focused on the risk of AI systems developing goals misaligned with humans. But as governments and militaries race to deploy AI, is the scarier scenario AI that works perfectly, just for the wrong people?

prompts.text: America's AI safety movement is just imperialism with better branding
prompts.subtitle: The US declined to back the second International AI Safety Report in February 2026, the largest global AI safety collaboration ever assembled. Simultaneously, the US frames chip export controls to China as a safety measure, while American VC-backed labs dominate the global narrative on what "responsible AI development" means.

prompts.text: AI will create more jobs than it destroys
AI safety guardrails are just corporate liability shields, not genuine ethics
prompts.subtitle: Governments are literally calling safety features 'virtue signaling.' Is safety research a moral project or a PR one?

prompts.text: AI systems should be required to be explainable before deployment in high-stakes domains
Universal Basic Income is necessary in a world with advanced AI
The benefits of AI will be distributed equitably across society
